summary: Flask debug mode is enabled by default because app.py:33 sets unfurl_debug='True' (a truthy string) as the default parameter. When no unfurl.ini config file exists (which is the case after pip install since MANIFEST.in does not include it), Flask runs with debug=True, exposing the Werkzeug interactive debugger console at /console.
expected_security_impact: The Werkzeug interactive debugger is accessible at /console, allowing unauthenticated remote code execution. An attacker can execute arbitrary Python code on the server.
actual_observed_result: Server log shows 'Debug mode: on', 'Debugger is active!', and 'Debugger PIN: 100-815-443'. HTTP GET /console returns 200 with the Werkzeug debugger UI. Server header contains 'Werkzeug'. Error responses expose traceback information.
verification_method: Automated: trigger_debug.py checks for debug mode indicators: (1) Werkzeug in Server header, (2) /console endpoint accessible with debugger UI, (3) error responses containing traceback/debugger content, (4) debugger secret extraction. Success requires at least 2 indicators.
evidence_produced_by_run: GET /console returned HTTP 200 with Werkzeug debugger UI
limitations_or_blockers: The PoC demonstrates debug mode activation and debugger console accessibility but does not execute arbitrary code through the console to avoid ethical concerns
Werkzeug debugger requires a PIN for console execution in newer versions, but the PIN is derived from stable system properties and can be brute-forced
The vulnerability requires the Flask web UI to be running (not the CLI tool)

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit or ref, and mounts that checkout into the container.
